name: Hong Kong
localTips: Get an Octopus card — it works on the MTR, trams, buses, ferries and even 7-Eleven purchases; just tap in and out everywhere.
Stand on the right on MTR escalators — Hong Kong is one of the few Asian cities where this etiquette is strictly enforced.
Typhoon signals above Signal 8 shut the city down — check the Hong Kong Observatory app if the sky looks ominous, since buses and ferries stop running.
Tipping isn't obligatory, but restaurants often add a 10% service charge automatically — check the bill before leaving extra.
Cash is still king at wet markets, dai pai dong stalls and some older restaurants — carry small HKD notes even though cards work almost everywhere else.

activities.title: Victoria Peak hiking loop
activities.description: Skip the tram queue and walk up Old Peak Road, then loop the flat Lugard Road circuit for a free harbour panorama that rivals the paid Sky Terrace.

activities.title: Star Ferry harbour crossing
activities.description: A century-old green-and-white ferry crosses Victoria Harbour between Tsim Sha Tsui and Central for a couple of Hong Kong dollars — still the cheapest great view in the city.

activities.title: Avenue of Stars promenade
activities.description: A waterfront walk honouring Hong Kong cinema, Bruce Lee statue included, facing the skyscraper wall of Hong Kong Island — packed for the nightly Symphony of Lights.

activities.title: Man Mo Temple
activities.description: Giant incense coils spiral from the ceiling of this 1847 temple dedicated to the gods of literature and war — one of the oldest and most atmospheric in the city.

activities.title: Tai O fishing village
activities.description: Stilt houses built by the Tanka fishing community line the waterways of this Lantau Island village — wander the plank walkways past dried-seafood stalls.

activities.title: Sai Kung waterfront
activities.description: A laid-back fishing-town promenade lined with seafood tanks and junk boats for hire — the gateway to Hong Kong's least-visited country parks and beaches.

activities.title: Dragon's Back ridge hike
activities.description: A rolling ridgeline trail above Shek O, once named 'best urban hike in Asia' by Time — beach, forest and skyline views within an hour of the MTR.

activities.title: PMQ creative hub
activities.description: Former police married quarters turned design studios and boutiques — browse local fashion and homeware labels across two blocks of restored 1950s architecture.

activities.title: Central–Mid-Levels Escalator
activities.description: The world's longest outdoor covered escalator system, 800 metres climbing through Soho and Central's back streets — hop off anywhere for street food or bars.

activities.title: Temple Street Night Market
activities.description: Fortune tellers, Cantonese opera singers and stalls of knock-off goods fill this covered-canopy market after dusk — order clay-pot rice from the street kitchens along the edge.

activities.title: Ladies Market Mong Kok
activities.description: A kilometre of open-air stalls selling clothes, accessories and souvenirs — haggling is expected and prices drop fast once you start walking away.

activities.title: Choi Hung Estate rainbow blocks
activities.description: A 1960s public housing estate painted in candy-coloured stripes, its rooftop basketball court framed by the rainbow facade — one of the city's most photographed spots.

activities.title: Nan Lian Garden & Chi Lin Nunnery
activities.description: A Tang-dynasty-style garden of black pines and koi ponds beside a wooden nunnery complex built without a single nail — a silent, cypress-scented break from the city.

activities.title: Symphony of Lights show
activities.description: Skyscrapers on both sides of Victoria Harbour synchronise lasers and searchlights to music every night at 8pm — watch free from the Avenue of Stars or Golden Bauhinia Square.

activities.title: Hong Kong Park & aviary
activities.description: A walk-through aviary of over 80 bird species inside a landscaped park wedged between Central's skyscrapers — waterfalls, a conservatory and a tai chi garden at dawn.

activities.title: Classic cha chaan teng breakfast
activities.description: Milk tea pulled through a stocking, pork chop buns and eggs-on-toast at institutions like Australia Dairy Company or Mido Café — Hong Kong's diner culture since the 1950s.
activities.insiderNote: Sharing tables is normal and expected at busy cha chaan teng — don't wait for a table to yourself, just sit where there's a free seat.

activities.title: Wonton noodle institution
activities.description: Mak's Noodle and Tsim Chai Kee have argued over Hong Kong's best bowl of shrimp wonton noodles for decades — order the small size, it's still a full portion.
activities.insiderNote: Ask for 'gon lo' (dry, sauce-tossed) instead of soup if you want the noodles at their springiest — a local order most tourists don't know to ask for.

activities.title: Dai pai dong street stall dinner
activities.description: Open-air licensed food stalls with battered metal stools and roaring woks — a dying breed, with the best surviving cluster on Stanley Street and in Sham Shui Po.
activities.insiderNote: Look for the green-and-white striped canvas awnings, the only licensing colour scheme still permitted — most of the originals have closed since the 1970s freeze on new licences.

activities.title: Ding Ding double-decker tram ride
activities.description: The 1904 tram network still crawls along Hong Kong Island's north shore for a flat fare — ride the upper deck front row from Western Market to Causeway Bay.
activities.insiderNote: It's the slowest way across the island and exactly the point — pay on exit with your Octopus card and grab the very front seat upstairs if it's free.

activities.title: Wet market cooked food centre
activities.description: Upper floors of municipal wet markets like Java Road or Kowloon City run rows of no-frills kitchens — bring your own catch from the fishmonger below and they'll cook it.
activities.insiderNote: Java Road Cooked Food Centre in North Point is a local favourite for beer and typhoon-shelter crab, with almost no English signage.

activities.title: Sham Shui Po fabric streets & neon
activities.description: Wholesale fabric and electronics streets by day give way to some of the city's last surviving hand-painted neon signs by night — bring a wide-angle lens for Apliu Street.
activities.insiderNote: The neon sign preservation project has mapped which surviving signs are still lit — Google 'Sham Shui Po neon walk' for a self-guided route.

activities.title: Hidden rooftop bar
activities.description: Unmarked rooftop bars above Wan Chai and Sheung Wan shopfronts offer harbour-adjacent views without the hotel bar markup — Ozone and Sugar are famous, but smaller ones stay quiet.
activities.insiderNote: Ask bartenders in Tai Ping Shan Street cafes for the current 'secret' rooftop of the month — the scene shifts as buildings enforce noise complaints.

activities.title: Traditional herbal tea shop
activities.description: Bitter 24-flavour herbal tea (leung cha) served from giant copper pots at century-old shops like Kung Lee — a Cantonese remedy shot for whatever ails you.
activities.insiderNote: It's meant to be drunk in one go, standing at the counter — a preserved plum afterward cuts the bitterness, the traditional chaser.

activities.title: Peak Tram & Sky Terrace 428
activities.description: The 1888 funicular climbs a near-vertical track to the Peak Tower's rooftop viewing deck — book a timed ticket to skip the queue that snakes down Garden Road.
activities.bookingQuery: Peak Tram Sky Terrace 428 ticket Hong Kong

activities.title: Ngong Ping 360 cable car & Big Buddha
activities.description: A 25-minute glass-bottomed cable car ride over Lantau's hills ends at the 34-metre bronze Tian Tan Buddha and the Po Lin Monastery's vegetarian temple kitchen.
activities.bookingQuery: Ngong Ping 360 cable car Big Buddha ticket Hong Kong

activities.title: Victoria Harbour light show cruise
activities.description: A junk boat or catamaran positions you mid-harbour for the 8pm Symphony of Lights, drink in hand, instead of jostling for a rail spot on the promenade.
activities.bookingQuery: Victoria Harbour Symphony of Lights cruise Hong Kong

activities.title: Junk boat trip to the outer islands
activities.description: Charter a traditional red-sailed junk from Sai Kung or Aberdeen out to a swimmable bay — a barbecue, beer and a much quieter Hong Kong than the skyline suggests.
activities.bookingQuery: junk boat day trip Hong Kong

activities.title: Dim sum cooking class
activities.description: Learn to fold har gow and shape siu mai with a chef in a hands-on class, then eat everything you made alongside tea-pairing etiquette lessons.
activities.bookingQuery: dim sum cooking class Hong Kong

activities.title: Ocean Park
activities.description: A hillside marine park with a cable car over the headland, giant panda enclosure and a thrill-ride zone perched on the clifftop above the South China Sea.
activities.bookingQuery: Ocean Park Hong Kong admission ticket

activities.title: Hong Kong Disneyland
activities.description: The smallest Disney park worldwide, easy to cover in a day — the only one with a dedicated Iron Man Experience ride and a Mystic Manor attraction unique to this park.

activities.title: Tai O pink dolphin boat tour
activities.description: A small motorboat winds through Tai O's stilt-house canals before heading out to open water for a chance to spot the rare Chinese white (pink) dolphin.
activities.bookingQuery: Tai O pink dolphin boat tour Hong Kong

activities.title: Egg tart baking class
activities.description: A bakery-run class teaches the flaky Portuguese-Cantonese hybrid pastry that Hong Kong claims as its own — box up a dozen warm tarts to take home.
activities.bookingQuery: egg tart baking class Hong Kong

activities.title: Helicopter tour over Victoria Harbour
activities.description: A 10–15 minute helicopter loop over the skyscraper canyon and harbour — the fastest way to see how tightly Hong Kong is stacked between mountain and sea.
activities.bookingQuery: helicopter tour Victoria Harbour Hong Kong
